meaning: n.自夸,值得夸耀的事物v.自夸,以有...而自豪
examples: All three Internet agencies boast they've won major accounts.
I don't want to boast, but I can actually speak six languages.
In its heyday, the studio's boast was that it had more stars than there are in heaven.
Executives in Round Rock boast about holding the operating margin almost constant amid a vicious price war.
But, Ron Paul aside, the Democrats boast that they have youth on their side in droves.
